title: What took the biggest toll on Shinji
display_title: What took the biggest toll on Shinji
wikitext:
[]: What took the biggest toll on Shinji?             Quote Post by Trowa_Ayanami » Sat Nov 05, 2005 10:03 am During all the events that happened in the Evangelion series and End of Evangelion, which moment took the biggest toll on Shinji? Here are your choices: Seeing Touji getting crippled in Ambivilance. Misato's cold words. Asuka's depressed suicidal state. Rei's death. Seeing the clones of Rei in the Chamber of Gulf. Touji and Kensuke leaving. Forced to kill Kaworu, his only best friend he had. Death of Misato. Seeing Unit 02's mangled corpse from the Eva series. If I chosen one, I'd say when he saw Touji in Unit 03 because it was the starting point of the many bad things that would happen to him throughout the series. What's your opinion?

[]:             Quote Post by ThroneofDravaris » Sat Nov 05, 2005 10:13 am You forgot the guilt he felt after masturbating over Asukaâ€™s comatose body. I would say killing Kaworu sent him over the edge more than anything elseâ€¦

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Nov 05, 2005 11:55 am Yui being absorbed into unit one before his eyes was the worst Toji was proby second I think we have a tie for third with Misato not telling him about Toji, as she was his only real suport, and gendo making him do nothing but listen while Asuka was mind raped. The spankstrobation bit in EoE im goiong to pass on he was already broken by that point.

[]:             Quote Post by Six Winged Angel » Sat Nov 05, 2005 12:53 pm yes the worst is toji ( :cry: poor litle evangelion 03 :cry: ) and then comes Kaworu, the deathblow :P

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Nov 05, 2005 5:23 pm Kaworu is clearly the single most devestating incident. However, don't underestimate the stress induced by Asuka's relentless tirades.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Nov 05, 2005 5:27 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Kaworu is clearly the single most devestating incident. However, don't underestimate the stress induced by Asuka's relentless tirades.

[]:             Quote Post by Heidroch » Sat Nov 05, 2005 6:02 pm The killing of Kawarou. Throughout the entire series, the only person who ever told Shinji "I like/love you", was Kawarou. I'm sure I don't need to explain how much something like that meant to Shinji. Then Shinji has to kill him. I'm pretty sure that was the thing that really hit the hardest.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Nov 05, 2005 8:31 pm Heidroch wrote: The killing of Kawarou. Throughout the entire series, the only person who ever told Shinji "I like/love you", was Kawarou. I'm sure I don't need to explain how much something like that meant to Shinji. Then Shinji has to kill him. I'm pretty sure that was the thing that really hit the hardest.

[]:             Quote Post by Heidroch » Sat Nov 05, 2005 11:00 pm *Smacks DatDude with bass guitar* I was agreeing with you, mate. We are both saying that Kawarou's death by Shinji's hands was indeed the "straw that broke the camels back", so to speak. Yet keep in mind; situations that "break the camels back" are usually the most intense for anyone. I would however like to comment on your statement of Misato's "warming up to Shinji": I don't think that Shinji ever really did warm up to Misato. Sure, they lived together, but they never had a genuine connecting scene, except for episode 2's "I'm home.... Welcome home"(EoE aside). Do remember the last episodes of the series, however: In episode 23, after Rei II's death and before Kawarou's episode 24 arrival, Misato does attempt to comfort him. Shinji answers with something similar to "don't touch me! Just.. leave me alone, Misato!", and she leaves. Also, at the end of episode 24, the last lines are Shinji asking: "Misato, how can you be so cruel?" Kawarou really hit a chord with Shinji, and vice versa. I'm not speaking on a romantic/shipping level, but I do believe that Shinji is at his happiest when he is around Kawarou. Also keep in mind that Kawarou is still the only character to ever say "I love you" to Shinji, which is utlimately what Shinji wanted from anyone. This makes it all more mentally traumatizing when Shinji kills him.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sun Nov 06, 2005 1:42 am Sorry :oops: As for the Misato Shinji relation ship I don't agree, but in tired and in need of a shower so im going to back out of this one for tonight. Laters

[]:             Quote Post by sachiel » Sun Nov 06, 2005 7:44 am I wouldn't say Kaworu was the only friend he had. What about Toji and Kensuke? They were friends. Just not...THAT friendly... I think everyone's sort of forgetting when he choked Asuka? That was pretty traumatizing...and when he's yelling at Misato, it's right up there on the list of horrible things he's done to people. "I did something horrible to Asuka, I killed Kaworu..." That and Kaworu's death probably took the largest toll on him, in my opinion.

[]:             Quote Post by Eternal Yamcha » Sun Nov 06, 2005 8:14 am Yet keep in mind; situations that "break the camels back" are usually the most intense for anyone.

[]:             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Sun Nov 06, 2005 10:08 am Killing Kawouru killed a part of Shinji. It killed 'affection' from his heart. Since Kawouru showed affection for Shinji, he thought things got a little better after meeting Kawouru-kun. But, when he found out Kawouru was an Angel... It killed Shinji. He hated himself after killing Kawouru. So the death of Kawouru-kun took the biggest toll on Shinji. The 2nd worst toll would be the death of Asuka in Nigouki. When I saw it, I started to scream unbarebly. I was scared... I felt a side of scaredeness. I felt like Shinji. It was too hard on Shinji, so he did absolutely nothing in Shigouki.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Nov 06, 2005 1:13 pm The activation test with Yui. I mean, come on. Itâ€™s the reason the show happens at all and it (unconsciously) dominates his life.

[]:             Quote Post by Shnooks » Sun Nov 06, 2005 3:30 pm YuiYuiYui. The Dissapearance of Yui Ikari. Isn't it pretty much what made him who he is?

[]:             Quote Post by Heidroch » Sun Nov 06, 2005 3:35 pm Of course! Yui's activation test with Unit 1. How did I not see it? The one event that took the biggest toll on both Shinji and Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by Eternal Yamcha » Sun Nov 06, 2005 11:04 pm Yeah, I guess Yui's disappearnce would be the biggest. I don't know, it seems so... Subtle to me. Then again, I would have no idea how hard that it is, to be honest.

[]:             Quote Post by defiate » Mon Nov 07, 2005 11:51 am new member here; long time eva fan (nearly a goddamn decade) just wana nod my head to heidroch... yui's disappearance is a major source of Shinji's pain, whether it is directly or indirectly, but i wouldn't go as far as saying that it took the biggest toll on the poor fella the direct pain comes from growing up without a mother, or parents for a matter of fact (wonder what Shinji would be like if he grew up with gendou... :roll: ) and the pain he went through is evidenced in EoE during the scene where he is playing with 2 other dolls in a sandbox with a background of serious freudian imagery the indirect pain would be from gendou, who changes after yui is lost in the contact experiment (episode 21, "He was aware that he was still a child"). if yui had still been around whilst the series started, Shinji would have undoubtedly been a much different kid (too bad yui couldnt protect him within eva-01 if that was the case, and there would be a LOT less to discuss in this forum in general. cant imagine evangelion without yui "supermom" ikari)

[]:             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Tue Nov 08, 2005 4:40 pm Shnooks wrote: YuiYuiYui. The Dissapearance of Yui Ikari. Isn't it pretty much what made him who he is?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Nov 08, 2005 6:10 pm I think Gendou's abandonment more so that Yui's death was the biggest long term problem Shinji had. Kaworu's death was the single greatest blow however. You must remember that Shinji was the one who killed him, something he would not even risk doing in episode #18, even at the peril of Tokyo-3. To kill another person was crushing blow to Shinji. In a sense, he had truely crossed the Rubicon, never to return.

Here are your choices: Seeing Touji getting crippled in Ambivilance. Misato's cold words. Asuka's depressed suicidal state. Rei's death. Seeing the clones of Rei in the Chamber of Gulf. Touji
